algorithms.title: ACLS Tachycardia With a Pulse Algorithm
algorithms.description: Complete decision pathway for adult tachycardia with a pulse, from initial assessment through rhythm-specific treatment.
steps: Tachycardia identified (heart rate >100/min with a pulse) on the monitor.
Assess for hemodynamic instability: hypotension (SBP <90), acutely altered mental status, chest pain, acute heart failure, signs of shock.
UNSTABLE → Immediate synchronized cardioversion.
Sedate if conscious and time permits (midazolam, etomidate, or propofol).
Energy: 100 J biphasic for regular narrow-complex or regular monomorphic VT.
Energy: ≥200 J biphasic for atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter.
If polymorphic VT → UNSYNCHRONIZED defibrillation (synchronization contraindicated).
STABLE → Identify the rhythm category.
NARROW-COMPLEX, REGULAR (most likely SVT):
Step 1: Vagal maneuvers (modified Valsalva, carotid sinus massage).
Step 2: Adenosine 6 mg rapid IV push (proximal port + rapid saline flush).
Step 3: Adenosine 12 mg (may repeat once).
Step 4: If still refractory: beta-blocker or calcium-channel blocker.
NARROW-COMPLEX, IRREGULAR (AF / atrial flutter with variable block):
Rate control: diltiazem, metoprolol, or amiodarone.
Target resting HR <110/min (lenient) or <80/min (strict).
Consider anticoagulation per stroke-risk guidelines.
WIDE-COMPLEX, REGULAR (assume VT):
Amiodarone 150 mg IV over 10 minutes (may repeat once).
Alternative: Procainamide 20–50 mg/min IV.
If becomes unstable → synchronized cardioversion at 100 J.
WIDE-COMPLEX, IRREGULAR:
If polymorphic VT → unsynchronized defibrillation + magnesium 1–2 g IV.
If pre-excited AF (WPW) → avoid AV-nodal blockers; consider procainamide or cardioversion.
In parallel: identify and treat reversible causes (hypoxia, electrolytes, toxins, ischemia).

algorithms.title: Synchronized Cardioversion Quick-Reference
algorithms.description: Energy selection and preparation checklist for synchronized cardioversion.
steps: Confirm tachycardia is UNSTABLE (hypotension, altered mentation, chest pain, heart failure, shock).
Identify the rhythm: narrow vs wide, regular vs irregular.
Set defibrillator to SYNCHRONIZED mode (confirm R-wave tracking).
Select energy based on rhythm: regular narrow (SVT) = 100 J; regular monomorphic VT = 100 J; AF/AFlutter = ≥200 J (biphasic).
Sedate the patient if conscious and time permits (midazolam, etomidate, propofol).
Announce 'synchronized cardioversion — everyone clear' and deliver the shock.
Immediately reassess rhythm, blood pressure, and clinical status.
If rhythm persists: increase energy and repeat, or add antiarrhythmic (amiodarone) between shocks.
EXCEPTION: If the rhythm converts to polymorphic VT, immediately switch to UNSYNCHRONIZED defibrillation.
Post-procedure: monitor, identify and treat reversible causes, continue ACLS if needed.
